_comment: E8 ROOT SYSTEM — The 240 Roots
E8 is a rank-8 root system with 240 roots in 8-dimensional
Euclidean space. There are exactly two types of roots:
D8 (integer type): All coordinates are integers.
Form: permutations of (±1, ±1, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0)
S+ (half-integer/spinor type): All coordinates are ±½.
Form: (±½, ±½, ±½, ±½, ±½, ±½, ±½, ±½)
Constraint: even number of minus signs
Count: 2⁸ / 2 = 128
Every root has squared norm 2 (they all lie on a sphere).
This file contains all 240 roots with their coordinates,
type classification, and shell assignment under the
stereoscopic parity projection.
Under the stereoscopic parity projection, the 240 roots
fall into 6 concentric shells with the following populations.
Shell radii satisfy: r²_k = 1 + k × δ, where δ = √5/10.
The golden ratio √5 emerges from the E8 Coxeter eigenvalues.
The palindrome [24, 56, 40, 40, 56, 24] reads the same
forwards and backwards — a mirror symmetry connecting
matter and antimatter representations.
